text: Feline Immunodeficiency Virus (FIV) and Feline Leukemia Virus (FeLV) are Retroviruses that affect domestic and wild host cats world-wide and have a major impact on the health and welfare of the host animal. The causative Retroviruses lead to disease and a compromised quality of life for the affected animal which results in a decrease in its life span. This study looks at FIV and FeLV prevalence among cats sheltered in Portugal; investigating the burden of the found Retroviral infections. Blood samples were collected (n=326) during routine examination upon entry in a Portuguese animal shelter. The blood samples were thereafter tested using an Immunochromatography kit in order to determine the presence of FIV and/or FeLV. Upon completion of the tests, it was established that the overall seroprevalance of the Retrovirus was 15.3% or 50/326, (95% CI; 11.6–19.7). When the individual Retrovirus samples were expressed as positive, a prevalence of 9.8% was found for FIV, (95% CI; 6.8-13.6), for FeLV a prevalence of 2.1%, (95% CI; 0.9–4.4) was recorded, whilst for co-infection with both FIV and FeLV the prevalence was 3.4%, (95% CI; 1.7–6.0). Higher prevalence rates of FIV as well as co-infection with FIV and FeLV was observed in the adult population of the animals tested in this research study. It was also recorded that neither sex, breed, hair, nor the municipality in which it was located showed any statistical relation to a higher prevalence of FeLV, FIV or the co-infection of FIV and FeLV within the population of the animals in this research study. The found prevalence of the Retroviruses is a first for sheltered cats in Portugal. This is a groundbreaking study which increases the knowledge as to whether FIV and FeLV Retroviruses are endemic in Portugal, as well as the fact that adherence to the national vaccination regime is poor as well as the control measures implemented for the Retrovirus’s in the feline population in Portugal, which has to be addressed in order to protect both animal as well as human health.
explanation: The text uses solid scientific structure and concrete data, but includes awkward phrasing and promotional language ('groundbreaking study') that are common in AI-generated writing. Because human-authored abstracts can also display these traits, the assessment is uncertain but leans toward AI involvement.
